pests.name: Whitefly
pests.impact: Acts as a vector for Leaf Curl Virus, causing pale yellow leaves, prominent veins, stunted growth, reduced leaf size, upward curling, and small fruits.
pests.solution: Use high-quality seeds and maintain nurseries properly, start cultivation at the right time, establish windbreaks, destroy host plants, immediately remove and destroy diseased plants, control vectors.

diseases.name: Damping Off
diseases.impact: Seedlings die before emergence (pre-emergence damping off), seeds and seedlings rot, post-emergence damping off affects the base or roots, infected tissues show wet and soft rot, severe damage causes shrinking and collapse, leaves wilt before collapse, seedlings die in patches.
diseases.solution: Establish nurseries in well-drained areas not previously cultivated with Solanaceae crops (tomato, eggplant, chili), crop rotation with coarse grain crops, nursery sterilization (burning, solarization), avoid high seed density in nursery (40g seeds for 3m x 1m bed), remove and destroy infected plants with soil.

diseases.name: Anthracnose
diseases.impact: Large spots on young pods causing them to drop, small circular sunken spots on fruits spreading longitudinally, symptoms on leaves, stems, and fruits, discoloration of peduncles, flower buds, and shoots, flower drop, brown circular spots on ripe pods, rotting of ripe pods, severe damage causes dieback from the stem apex (dieback during growth stage), severe damage spreads throughout the plant and causes death.
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds, field sanitation (destroy crop residues), seed treatment, remove diseased plant parts from the field, crop rotation with non-host crops every 2-3 years, maintain recommended plant spacing, ensure good field drainage, use resistant varieties, use Trichoderma species, Pseudomonas fluorescens.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Individual plants or patches of plants wilt in the field, immature leaves wilt during the hottest part of the day, followed by rapid permanent wilting, plants die a few days after defoliation, a white bacterial ooze from the cut surface when the stem near the soil level is cut and placed in water.
diseases.solution: Crop rotation with non-host crops, use resistant varieties, remove and destroy all host plants and infected roots, remove and destroy infected plants with surrounding soil, prevent irrigation from infected fields to healthy fields, maintain good field drainage.

diseases.name: Leaf Curl Virus Disease
diseases.impact: Leaves turn pale yellow and veins become prominent, internodes shorten, excessive branching leads to stunted plants, reduced leaf size causes upward curling, small fruits develop.
diseases.solution: Use high-quality seeds and maintain nurseries properly, start cultivation at the right time, establish windbreaks, destroy host plants, immediately remove and destroy diseased plants, control vectors.

diseases.name: Leaf Spot Disease
diseases.impact: Brown leaf spots with a pale gray center and a yellow halo, leaves turn yellow and drop, infected leaves roll, wilt, and drop, severe leaf drop during high humidity, sometimes dark brown spots on petioles, pods, or branches, mature lower leaves are infected first, upper leaves are infected in severe cases, infected crop residues, temperatures of 20-25°C, and high humidity are favorable conditions.
diseases.solution: Collect and destroy infected leaves, prevent high humidity by using recommended spacing and water management, apply organic fertilizers properly.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Pale yellow spots appear on the leaf surface, small powdery spots on the underside of leaves spread rapidly and become large, leaves gradually turn yellow and drop, in severe cases, leaves become covered with a white powdery substance.
diseases.solution: Field sanitation, ensure good air circulation around crops by maintaining proper spacing and weed control, use Trichoderma spp.

diseases.name: Choanephora Blight
diseases.impact: Symptoms first appear on flowers, flower buds, and apical parts of the plant, infected parts turn brown or black, fungus rapidly grows downwards, and upper parts die, wet rot is observed, spore structures are visible to the naked eye, infected parts appear grayish-silver, pod infection starts from the sepals, infection can occur from seedling to flowering stage, infections can occur even without insect or mechanical wounds, but wounds facilitate entry and infection.
diseases.solution: Maintain recommended spacing, immediately remove and destroy diseased parts upon detection.
